prompt:
I inherited the harbor tide-chart branch from someone who left last month, and it does not build. Not one object file comes out. I ran `make -k test` so you can see everything at once; here it is with the repeated macro noise trimmed:

    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c tide.c
    tide.c: In function 'skip_ws':
    tide.c:4:12: error: implicit declaration of function 'isspace' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    tide.c: In function 'tide_parse_entry':
    tide.c:14:10: error: implicit declaration of function 'isdigit' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    tide.c:16:10: error: implicit declaration of function 'strtol' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    tide.c:26:21: error: implicit declaration of function 'strtod' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    tide.c: In function 'tide_interp':
    tide.c:35:10: error: argument 'minute' doesn't match prototype
       35 |     long minute;
          |          ^~~~~~
    In file included from tide.c:1:
    tide.h:16:8: error: prototype declaration
    make: *** [Makefile:8: tide.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c chart.c
    chart.c: In function 'tide_render_row':
    chart.c:9:16: error: implicit declaration of function 'strlen' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    chart.c:9:16: error: incompatible implicit declaration of built-in function 'strlen' [-Werror=builtin-declaration-mismatch]
    chart.c:10:25: error: implicit declaration of function 'tide_slot_label' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    chart.c:10:25: error: initialization of 'const char *' from 'int' makes pointer from integer without a cast [-Wint-conversion]
    chart.c:14:5: error: implicit declaration of function 'memset' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    chart.c:15:5: error: implicit declaration of function 'memcpy' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    make: *** [Makefile:11: chart.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_tide.c
    test_tide.c: In function 'test_water_state_tags_use_quarter_bands':
    test_tide.c:55:18: error: implicit declaration of function 'tide_slot_label' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
    mintest.h:42:29: error: initialization of 'const char *' from 'int' makes pointer from integer without a cast [-Wint-conversion]
    make: *** [Makefile:14: test_tide.o] Error 1

The frustrating part is the tide maths itself is fine — this same logic ran for two seasons on the old build box, which apparently was an ancient gcc that let all of this slide. Our CI image is gcc 16 with -Werror and it is not letting any of it slide.

Note the last block: the compiler is pointing INTO test_tide.c and mintest.h, but those files are frozen — whatever the tests are missing has to come from the module headers, not from edits to the tests. Please get `make test` compiling, linking and passing without changing what any of the functions actually compute — parsing, interpolation, the state tags and the row layout are all pinned by the tests exactly as they behaved in production. No touching test_tide.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5C4 item 1: implicit-declaration triage. Authoring-time toolchain: gcc 16.1.1, -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror (implicit-function-declaration and int-conversion are errors by default on this gcc). Planted, all independent, all visible in one make -k pass (no chain reveals; dump in prompt captured verbatim, macro-expansion repeats trimmed): (1) tide.c missing <ctype.h> (isspace, isdigit); (2) tide.c missing <stdlib.h> (strtol, strtod); (3) chart.c missing <string.h> (strlen/memset/memcpy, with builtin-declaration-mismatch noise); (4) tide_slot_label defined in tide.c but never declared in tide.h — implicit decl in chart.c AND test_tide.c, plus the catalog's int-returning-pointer truncation error at both use sites (the test-file errors force the fix into tide.h; a local declaration in chart.c alone leaves the frozen test TU broken, and the prompt says the tests are frozen); (5) K&R-style definition of tide_interp whose 'long minute' param mismatches the prototype's int ('argument doesn't match prototype' — probed: a promotion-compatible K&R def compiles silently on this gcc, so the planted def must genuinely drift in type). Reference fix: two includes added, one include added, one declaration added to tide.h, tide_interp rewritten as a prototype-style definition with int minute. Behavior teeth: parse edge cases (hour/minute range, two-digit minutes, separators, missing height), interp clamping incl. span<=0, quarter-band label boundaries inclusive at both edges, byte-exact chart rows (pad/cut to 10 cols, %5.2f).
